raw_output_payload.reasoning: Need find files where group has only read permission (i.e., group permissions are r--, no w or x). Use find with -perm. For group read only: mode 0400? Actually file perms: group bits: 4. So we want group bits equal to 4. Use -perm -0040? Wait -perm -0040 means group read set. But also need to ensure no group write or execute: -perm -0040 and ! -perm -0020 and ! -perm -0010. Or use -perm 0644? That includes owner read/write. But we just care group. Use -perm -0040 ! -perm -0020 ! -perm -0010. Also list all files. So command: find /workspace -type f -perm -0040 ! -perm -0020 ! -perm -0010 -printf "%p\n".

But also need to ensure no group write or execute: -perm -0040 and ! -perm -0020 and ! -perm -0010. Or use -perm 0644? That includes owner read/write. But we just care group. Use -perm -0040 ! -perm -0020 ! -perm -0010. Also list all files. So command: find /workspace -type f -perm -0040 ! -perm -0020 ! -perm -0010 -printf "%p\n".
